title: Bengaluru-Style Thatte Idli
description: Soft, fluffy steamed cakes made from fermented rice and urad dal batter. A traditional South Indian breakfast staple with a light, airy crumb.

adjustments.ingredient_note: Add 50g cooked rice while grinding rice for softer, moist idlis

subSteps: Measure 1000 g idli rice
Rinse under water 2-3 times
Add to a bowl with water to cover by 2 inches
Soak separately for 4-6 hours at room temperature
VALIDATION: Rice grains soft when pressed, water slightly cloudy after rinsing

preparation.instruction: Soak urad dal and methi seeds
subSteps: Measure 375 g whole urad dal
Measure 8 g fenugreek seeds
Combine in a separate bowl
Add water to cover by 2 inches
Soak separately for 4-6 hours at room temperature
VALIDATION: Dal grains soft, easily crushable between fingers
Measure 40 g poha
Add to a small bowl with water to cover
Soak for 15-20 minutes before grinding
VALIDATION: Poha softened, easily mashed by hand

preparation.instruction: Grind urad dal to fluffy batter
subSteps: Drain soaked urad dal and methi seeds completely
Transfer to wet grinder
Grind until very smooth, fluffy, and airy
Add water gradually (2-3 tbsp at a time) as needed
Grind for 10-15 minutes until batter becomes light and airy
VALIDATION: Batter is fluffy, light in color, holds peaks when lifted with spoon

preparation.instruction: Grind rice and poha to smooth batter
subSteps: Drain soaked rice completely
Drain soaked poha
Transfer both to wet grinder
Grind until smooth to slightly grainy texture
Add water gradually (2-3 tbsp at a time) as needed
Do not make completely coarse or too fine
VALIDATION: Batter is smooth, slightly grainy, not powdery, not paste-like
Pour urad batter into a large vessel or bowl
Add rice batter to the same vessel
Mix thoroughly using your hand for 2-3 minutes
Distribute urad batter evenly throughout rice batter
Leave at least 30-40% empty space in vessel for fermentation
VALIDATION: Uniform mixture, no pockets of separated batter, light and airy texture
Cover vessel loosely (do not seal tightly)
Leave at room temperature for 8-12 hours depending on temperature
Warmer environments ferment faster (8 hours), cooler takes longer (12 hours)
Do not stir or disturb during fermentation
VALIDATION: Batter rises noticeably, becomes airy, small bubbles visible on surface, mild fermented aroma develops
After fermentation completes, add 22-25 g salt
Mix gently using hand for 1-2 minutes
Do not over-mix or you will deflate the batter
VALIDATION: Salt evenly distributed, batter still light and airy, no loss of volume

ingredients.name: Idli Rice
ingredients.notes: Use short-grain rice specifically for idlis, not regular basmati

ingredients.name: Whole Urad Dal
ingredients.notes: Use whole urad dal (sabut urad), not split or husked

ingredients.name: Poha (Flattened Rice)
ingredients.notes: Adds softness and moisture to the idlis

ingredients.name: Fenugreek Seeds (Methi)
ingredients.notes: Aids fermentation and adds subtle flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Add after fermentation, adjust to taste (22-25 g)

subSteps: Pour 2 cups water into 10-litre pressure cooker
Place cooker on stove
Heat on medium flame until water begins to boil vigorously
VALIDATION: Large bubbles rising rapidly, steam visible, no boiling over

steps.instruction: Prepare and grease thatte idli plates
subSteps: Take thatte idli plates (6-inch)
Lightly grease each plate with oil or ghee using fingers or brush
Spread oil evenly across entire surface
Ensure grooves and edges are greased
VALIDATION: Entire plate has thin, even oil coating, no dry spots
Stir fermented batter gently once with hand
Use a ladle to pour batter into each greased plate
Fill each plate about ¾ full (three-quarters)
Do not fill to the brim to allow space for expansion
Repeat for all plates needed
VALIDATION: All plates uniformly filled, batter level is same across plates, no spills on edges
Arrange filled plates on idli stand in cooker
Stack plates if space allows
Carefully place entire stand with loaded plates inside hot cooker
Close cooker lid securely
Keep whistle (weight) on the cooker
VALIDATION: Stand is stable, plates do not tilt, lid closes completely, whistle is in place
Maintain medium flame throughout cooking
Wait for cooker to come to pressure and produce 1 whistle
Count approximately 1 minute after first whistle
Immediately switch off the stove when 1 whistle is complete
Do not allow a second whistle
VALIDATION: Single clear whistle sound, steam escaping from vent, no hissing continues
Leave cooker closed after switching off heat
Do not open lid or remove whistle
Allow residual steam to continue cooking for 5 minutes
This resting period improves softness and fluffiness
VALIDATION: Steam stops escaping from vent, cooker cools slightly, no pressure remaining
Open cooker lid carefully, tilting away from face
Allow steam to escape safely
Remove idli stand with filled plates
Let plates cool for 1-2 minutes
Use flat spoon, butter knife, or wet fingers to gently run around edges of each idli
Lift idli carefully from plate
Place on serving plate
VALIDATION: Idlis are soft, fluffy, light golden color, release easily without breaking
Arrange idlis on a plate
Serve immediately while hot and soft
Accompany with groundnut-coriander chutney
Serve with coconut chutney on the side
Pour sambar in a small bowl
Optional: drizzle small spoon of ghee on top
VALIDATION: Idlis are hot to touch, aroma is fresh and inviting, texture is light and bouncy

seo.metaTitle: Thatte Idli Recipe — Soft Bengaluru-Style Idlis
seo.metaDescription: Traditional Bengaluru-style thatte idli recipe. Learn to make soft, fluffy steamed idlis from fermented rice and urad dal batter with detailed instructions.
